summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds the UDR from source, starts it with a default configuration that uses HTTP and no NRF, and demonstrates that API requests without any Authorization header are accepted rather than rejected with 401 Unauthorized.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated HTTP GET request to the authentication subscription endpoint should be accepted (returning 200 or 404 depending on data presence) rather than rejected with 401 Unauthorized, proving the authentication bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the UDR binary, started MongoDB, started the UDR server (which logged NRF registration failure but continued serving), sent unauthenticated GET and POST requests to SBI endpoints, and received non-401 responses confirming the authentication bypass.
verification_method: The verify script sends two HTTP requests without an Authorization header: (1) GET /nudr-dr/v2/subscription-data/imsi-001010000000001/00101/authentication-subscription to check if the endpoint returns 200 or 404 (data not found) instead of 401, and (2) POST /nudr-dr/v2/subscription-data/subs-to-notify with a subscription body to verify write access is also unauthenticated. The script checks that neither response is HTTP 401, confirming that the auth middleware authorized the requests despite the missing token.
evidence_produced_by_run: UDR server logs show 'register to NRF failed' confirming OAuth2Required was never set to true.
UDR server logs show 'OAuth2 not required' debug message from AuthorizationCheck.
HTTP response to unauthenticated GET request was 404 (data not found in empty DB) rather than 401, proving the request was authorized.
HTTP response to unauthenticated POST request was 201 (subscription created) rather than 401, proving write access is also unauthenticated.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
